description: RAPP Agent Registry — headless RAPP + X™ API. Static reads come from main; authenticated CRUD commands use GitHub Issues as the durable control and audit surface. UI is optional.

registry.description: Full agent registry. Returns all agents with manifests, SHA256 hashes, and metadata.

agent_file.description: Download a single agent file. The file IS the package.

cards.description: All minted cards with types, stats, abilities, SVG art, and seeds.

api_manifest.description: This file. The API discovery endpoint.

skill.description: Machine-readable skill interface. Full documentation for autonomous agents.

submit_agent.description: Compatibility alias for create/update. New clients should send a rar-change-request/1.0 command.
submit_agent.notes: The GitHub issue author is the submitting identity. Publisher namespace must match that authenticated GitHub account or a maintainer-curated delegation. Issue titles never grant ownership.

read_agent.description: Read active agents, content hashes, lifecycle, receipt IDs, and tombstones from the main-branch projection.

request_read_agent.description: Create an auditable read request. The processor comments current hash/lifecycle and closes the Issue without mutating main.

update_agent.description: Request replacement of an active agent. The current registry SHA256 is mandatory as if_match.

delete_agent.description: Request removal of an active agent. Publication history remains and a lifecycle tombstone is written.

restore_agent.description: Request restoration of a tombstoned agent with a semantically higher version and fresh source hash.

request_status.description: Read the request state. Machine receipt comments and labels project submitted, pending-review, notarized, deleted, failed, or rejected.

resolve_card_from_seed.description: Resolve a card from a numeric seed. Zero bandwidth. Use the seed_hash + mulberry32 algorithm from rapp_sdk.py.
resolve_card_from_seed.algorithm: seed -> mulberry32 PRNG -> deterministic type, stats, abilities, rarity

mcp.description: MCP on-ramp. This static surface IS a rapp-static-mcp/1.0 catalog: catalog = registry.json, frames = agents/* pinned by _sha256 / _first_commit_sha. Any MCP host reads the catalog, fetches a content-addressed agent frame, and verifies-before-exec against the published hash. MCP is transport realizing 'Chat Is The Only Wire' — MCP clients are Layer 2 callers of a brainstem's /chat (via rapp_brainstem_mcp.py), not a new agent unit.
mcp.verify_before_exec: Pin each agent by registry _sha256 (and _first_commit_sha for first-seen provenance); recompute the SHA256 of the fetched agent file and refuse to exec on mismatch.

federation_catalog.description: Consolidated ecosystem catalog (rar-federation/1.0): rapplications (kody-w/RAPP_Store bundles), senses (kody-w/RAPP_Sense_Store output overlays), and portable skills (kody-w/rapp-skills, kody-w/rapp-claude-skills). Slim daily projection; home repos are authoritative. Verify each entry's sha256 before exec.

discussion_ratings.description: Community ratings snapshot (rar-discussion-ratings/1.0): per-agent upvotes (positive GitHub reactions only), comment counts, and the agent's Discussion URL. One Discussion per agent, title = agent name. Upvote by adding a positive reaction to the Discussion; comment by replying in the thread. Refreshed daily.

self_submission.description: Humans, agents, twins, rapplications, Brainstems, and neighborhood peers use the same GitHub Issues CRUD contract. The pipeline binds authenticated GitHub identity, canonical admitted source bytes, checks, approval, receipt, and main-branch commit.
steps: 1. Agent reads api.json to discover endpoints
2. Agent composes its own .py source with __manifest__
3. Caller POSTs a rar-change-request/1.0 envelope to GitHub Issues
4. Pipeline derives immutable GitHub identity, request ID, revision ID, and source hash
5. Exact bytes land under an issue-specific staging revision
6. Authorized maintainer approval binds only that revision
7. Checks pass, mutation commits to main, and an immutable RAR receipt is published
constraints: Publisher namespace must match the authenticated GitHub user or an explicit maintainer-curated delegation
All identifiers must be snake_case (no dashes)
quality_tier must be community or experimental for submissions
No eval(), exec(), subprocess, os.system(), or hardcoded secrets
Must inherit BasicAgent and implement perform() returning str

large_sources.description: GitHub Issue bodies are size-limited. For large single-file agents, omit source.content and provide a revision-pinned GitHub source.url plus the mandatory SHA256.
large_sources.sdk_behavior: rapp_sdk.py automatically creates an unlisted revision-pinned Gist when the encoded Issue command exceeds 50 KiB.

hashing.input: UTF-8 agent source after replacing every CRLF byte pair with LF. No other Unicode, whitespace, or newline normalization is performed.
hashing.rule: The source embedded or fetched by the processor is canonicalized before digest verification, staging, receipt generation, registry projection, federation, and installation checks.

offline.description: RAPP is local-first. Everything works offline.
offline.seed_resolution: Any numeric seed resolves to a full card (type, stats, abilities) with zero network. The algorithm is deterministic and public.
offline.git_clone: git clone = full registry + all agents + all cards. No server needed.
